rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HILL et al VS. DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 11, 2008, line 7, PLAINTIFFS DENNIS HILL AND MARTHA HILL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED BASED ON THE COURT'S ORDER OF DECEMBER 9, 2008. THE COURT DOES NOT INTEND TO ISSUE THE BROAD INJUNCTIVE RELIEF REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF, RATHER, THE COURT HAS DECLARED THE RIGHT OF THE PARTIES IN THIS CASE AND WILL ISSUE AN ORDER PRECLUDING DEFENDANT FROM ENFORCING ITS LIEN RIGHTS IN THIS CASE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HILL et al VS. DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 11, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON THE COURT'S ORDER OF DECEMBER 9, 2008. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE TORT CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT HAS ESTABLISHED THAT THE CONDUCT AT ISSUE IS PROTECTED UNDER CCP 425.16. DEFENDANT ALSO HAS DEMONSTRATED THAT THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, CIVIL CODE SECTION 47, IS APPLICABLE AND THAT IT HAS ACTED IN GOOD FAITH. SEE ACTION APARTMENT ASS'N, INC. V. CITY OF SANTA MONICA (2007) 41 CAL.APP.4TH 1231, 1251 AND BIRKNER V. LAM (2007) 156 CAL.APP.4TH 275, 286. PLAINTIFFS RELIANCE ON FLATLEY V. MAURO (2006) 39 CAL.4TH 229 IS MISPLACED GIVEN THE FACTS AT ISSUE. MILLER V. FILTER (2007) 150 CAL.APP.4TH 652. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MAPLE RABB VS. HILFASCO INCORPORATED DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE, A et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 11, 2008, line 8, DEFENDANT HILFASCO INCORPORATED DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE, JAMES CHIN, AND ORHAN TOLU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION, NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF CAN PLEAD FACTS THAT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ADMITS KNOWING OF THE 6% COMMISSION BEFORE CLOSING. HEARING REQUIRED IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS LEAVE TO AMEND. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STRAIN VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABIZATION AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Administrtive Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 11, 2008, line 9, PETITIONER KEVIN STRAIN'S Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus IS DENIED. SECTION 1.21 OF THE RENT ORDINANCE REQUIRES A REVIEW OF SEVERAL FACTORS TO DETERMINE A TENANTS PRINCIPAL RESIDENCE. THE PARTIES INTRODUCED EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THEIR RESPECTIVE POSITIONS AND THE RECORD IS MIXED WHEN THE ENUMERATED CRITERIA ARE CONSIDERED. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THE ADMINSTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S DECISION IS AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION UNDER THE SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE STANDARD. ALTHOUGH THE TENANT'S TAX RETURNS AND GRANT DEED ARE PROBATIVE, THE ADMINSTRATIVE LAW JUDGE PROPERLY GAVE WEIGHT TO CHILD SUPPORT DOCUMENTS, AND BANK DOCUMENTS AS WELL AS OTHER FACTORS CITED. =(302/PJM/JW)
